description: Two-process theory / discriminated avoidance (Solomon & Wynne, 1954): a warning CS precedes an aversive US; an operant response during the CS prevents (avoids) the US. The @avoidance annotation on the pair expression records the avoidance rule (response mode = cancel: response during CS cancels US for that trial).
input:
@cs(label="tone", duration=5s, modality="auditory")
@us(label="shock", intensity="0.8mA")

phase warm_up:
  sessions = 2
  VI30s

phase avoidance_training:
  sessions = 20
  Pair.ForwardDelay(tone, shock, isi=5s, cs_duration=5s) @avoidance(response="lever_press", mode="cancel")
_note: The @avoidance annotation is the declarative form of the response-contingent avoidance rule. An analyzer/executor pass interprets it: when a `response` occurs during the CS, the scheduled US is cancelled (mode=cancel) or its onset is postponed (mode=postpone; future). Fully expresses Solomon & Wynne (1954) discriminated avoidance.
